rationale: The citing paper does more than list this study as generic background. It uses Kishore et al. (2013) to support a specific hypothesis argument about performance heterogeneity under quota-based incentives (especially the claim about higher performers and extrinsic motivation) and cites it as a methodological precedent for quantile-regression analysis across the performance distribution. These roles inform both conceptual setup and empirical positioning of the bench-program study, even though the new paper does not directly test bonus versus commission contracts.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited field study as prior empirical grounding on how quota-based incentive plans affect different performer segments. It also references the cited paper as a precedent for examining heterogeneous effects via quantile-style analysis, then builds a distinct disincentive context around that foundation.
